original_post.title: рҹҡЁSay hello to the gang вқ—
original_post.body: Pinned5ScottВ NorthwolfJan 2 вҖўВ General рҹ’ӯрҹҡЁSay hello to the gang вқ—Welcome, brother!  Let's get started by introducing yourself.  Answer the following questions on your post:  1. Can you briefly share from A to Z how you got into self-improvement?  2. What was the pain that got you into self-improvement?  3. How did you get into self-improvement coaching/consulting?  4. How long have you've been a self-improvement coach?  5. At what stage of your journey are you? (no shame if you're just getting started, we all got to start somewhere).1659Last comment 6d ago

comment.author: DevonВ Hoyt
comment.content: WhatвҖҷs up everyone рҹ‘Ӣ glad to be here.My name is Devon.1. From A to Z вҖ” how I got into self-improvement:Life kept handing me wake-up calls. Health scares, financial stress, family responsibilities, and realizing that nobody was coming to save me. I started learning out of necessityвҖ”how systems work, how habits work, how mindset worksвҖ”because staying the same wasnвҖҷt an option anymore.2. The pain that pushed me:Feeling stuck while knowing I had people depending on me. That mix of frustration, fear, and responsibility is a powerful teacher. I didnвҖҷt want to just surviveвҖ”I wanted to become someone my kids could look up to.3. Coaching / consulting path:Not a formal coach yet. IвҖҷm more in the вҖңlearn it, apply it, share itвҖқ phase. I help people by breaking things down simply, sharing what IвҖҷve tested in real life, and being honest about what works and what doesnвҖҷt.4. How long IвҖҷve been in self-improvement:Several years of trial, error, setbacks, and course corrections. Still learning every day.5. Current stage of my journey:Actively building. Still sharpening habits, tightening discipline, and leveling up consistency. Not finishedвҖ”just committed.Looking forward to learning from everyone here and contributing where I can. рҹ‘Ҡ

replies.author: ScottВ Northwolf
replies.content: @Devon Hoyt epic, Devon! Thank you for sharing. Only when the pain of staying the same outweights the pain of facing the resistance and making a change is when we'll finally make a change. I heard from great men that the moment in which the truly became men was the very moment in which they realized nobody was coming to save them and they had to figure things out by themselves, it has been my experience as well.I'm really glad to have you here and looking forward to connect.

comment.author: AndreaВ Piras
comment.content: I got into self-improvement in the summer of 2022, almost 4 years ago. I did it because I had very few friends and life wasn't going as I wanted, so I decided to start meditating, journaling, exercising at home and various stuff, and I felt great. From that point I understood that it was lifestyle rather than a period of my life, so I have never stopped since then.I got into self-improvement coaching thanks to the Charlie Morgan's course (still watching it).I couldn't call myself a coach yet, since I haven't had any client yet, but I started following che course about a month ago.Right now I'm in a stage where I'm focusing on fixing my relationships and skills, and looking back I see that I have made a huge progress.

replies.author: ScottВ Northwolf
replies.content: @Andrea Piras you certainly have made insane progress, brother. IвҖҷm very proud of you. Stay on course.

comment.author: ArthurВ Maddocks-Brown
comment.content: Hey Guys!You may have seen my other post... so I PROMISE to keep this one shorter!After 15 years of depression and suicidal thoughts, the loss of my wife, house, dog, job, life, friends (everything) I hit rock bottom.I had a choice.Die or LiveIn that momentI chose to live..Not just EXISTBut Fucking LIVE!!!I studied life coaching and got 2 x certifications (Jay Shetty and Expert Coaching certifications)I got my breathwork, trauma and nervous system informed certificates as well as cold water immersion certification.When I started learning about coaching, in order to get my qualifications, I needed to coach people. I took what I learnt and started coaching (internationally)I fell in love with helping people. It made me feel good and it helped me heal.I've been in the coaching space now for 3 years!I am still in the building phase, but it's growing and opportunities are popping up.As soon as I got out my way... these things started to flow better.. I certainly could do better though!

comment.author: MiguelВ Ariza Lora
comment.content: What's up gang,My name is Miguel, 25 years old and Spanish-Dutch currently living in Thailand.My journey in self-improvement began around 15 years old when I was overweight. I really wanted to be more like the others my age. Luckily this is when YouTube began to take off and people starting sharing a lot of value around fitness and nutrition. Trial and error got me to losing 20kg's eventually, and I've been able to keep most of it off ever since. When I saw putting some work in could give me results, I realized the same thing could be done in other aspects.The following years I became a lot more extroverted, social and confident. Through studying social work and psychology I got into coaching people to build a better life. I was good doing those jobs, and now I've taken it online and moved to Thailand. Felt like a good next step in my self-improvement. Have been coaching people now for 7 years already. Learned a whole lot. About people, training techniques, but probably even more about myself.I just think the self-improvement journey is amazing, and I wish everyone can experience seeing themselves improve into a better version and better life, step by step. Does it ever end? I don't think so. There's always room for improvement. It can feel tiring at times, but I wouldn't want to have it any other way.Can't wait to connect more with all of you guys.- Miguel

This is truly were you belong. And now it's time to build something truly wonderful with all the wisdom we have accumulated and give back to the world that has been so abundantly generous to us.

comment.author: ThomasВ El Nadir
comment.content: Yo, Thomas here, soon-to-be 33 years old, French-Moroccan man currently living in Morocco.Can you briefly share from A to Z how you got into self-improvement?It all started at 17 when i wanted a girlfriend and googled "how to be more attractive to women" or something like that. I stumbled upon the "Game" community, the high achievers of the dating world, and oh boy it took me in quite the journey. This ultimately led me to understand how to attract and be charismatic not only from the outside but the inside, spirituality, and many epic adventures that got FAR beyond the dating world I initialy began with.What was the pain that got you into self-improvement?Loneliness and desire to connect (with women, at first).How did you get into self-improvement coaching/consulting?I went to more than a decade of life-changing experiences. Picked up many gems along the way that I realized with time would help so many people in the same struggle I was at 17 (and didn't go through what I went through to solve it).How long have you've been a self-improvement coach?It's been 7 years on-and-offAt what stage of your journey are you? (no shame if you're just getting started, we all got to start somewhere).Getting back to the coaching business with even more depth and background. And finally get rid of the shiny object syndrome that was such a curse of mine for that many years. Now that it's gone, i'm determined to build a powerful life for myself, and I have one more overcome to help people get out of to my arsenal ;)

comment.author: BenjaminВ S.
comment.content: (edited)Here's the promised prompt(btw if you want to change the theme/vibe, just change it from bali, indonesia to anything you want):Create a simple KPI tracking app with a tropical Bali, Indonesia vibe.Main goal:Build a clean, motivating KPI tracker that tracks only 4 business metrics, calculates them automatically, reverse-engineers the sales process, and adds light gamification.The 4 KPIs to track are:1. SCR = Sales Conversion Rate2. GFR = Good Fit Rate3. SUR = Show Up Rate4. ABR = Appointment Booking RateImportant rule:All KPI values must be treated as decimals internally and in the input fields.Examples:- 30% = 0.30- 50% = 0.50- 75% = 0.75The app should still display the percentage clearly for the user.Example:If SCR = 0.50, also show вҖң50%вҖқ--------------------------------------------------PART 1: KPI TRACKER--------------------------------------------------Create a simple section where I can enter raw numbers and the app calculates each KPI automatically.Use these formulas:- SCR = Clients Closed Г· Sales CallsExample:Sales Calls = 2Clients Closed = 1SCR = 0.50Display = 50%- GFR = Good Fit Calls Г· Show UpsExample:Show Ups = 10Good Fit Calls = 6GFR = 0.60Display = 60%- SUR = Show Ups Г· BookingsExample:Bookings = 8Show Ups = 4SUR = 0.50Display = 50%- ABR = Bookings Г· Outreach VolumeExample:Outreach Volume = 100Bookings = 20ABR = 0.20Display = 20%For each KPI card include:- KPI name- short formula- numerator input- denominator input- decimal result- percentage result- small tooltip/help textMake the tracker very simple and fast to use.--------------------------------------------------PART 2: REVERSE ENGINEERING CALCULATOR--------------------------------------------------The app must include a reverse-engineering planner using this exact formula chain:$ Goal Г· Client Value = Clients RequiredClients Required Г· SCR = Good Fit Calls RequiredGood Fit Calls Required Г· GFR = Show Ups RequiredShow Ups Required Г· SUR = Bookings RequiredBookings Required Г· ABR = Outreach Volume RequiredOutreach Volume Required Г· Days to Achieve Goal = Daily Activity RequiredImportant:- all metrics must be entered as decimals- example: 30% = 0.30- never interpret 30 as 30%- 0.30 is the valid input for 30%Inputs:- Revenue Goal ($)- Client Value ($)- SCR (decimal)- GFR (decimal)- SUR (decimal)- ABR (decimal)- Days to Achieve GoalOutputs:- Clients Required- Good Fit Calls Required- Show Ups Required- Bookings Required- Outreach Volume Required- Daily Activity RequiredRules:- round up all planning outputs where appropriate because in real life you canвҖҷt have part of a client or part of a call- update outputs instantly when inputs change- validate against division by zero- show friendly error messages- include helper text reminding the user that KPI inputs are decimals like 0.30--------------------------------------------------PART 3: GAMIFICATION--------------------------------------------------Gamify the app in a motivating but not childish way.Add a reward system with:- XP points- levels- weekly streak- monthly streakXP system ideas:- gain XP when entering data daily- gain XP for hitting or improving KPI benchmarks- gain XP for completing a weekly review- gain XP for maintaining streaksLevel system:Use tropical/Bali-inspired progression names such as:- Traveler- Explorer- Island Builder- Wave Rider- Temple Guardian- Master of the IslandShow:- current level- XP bar- XP needed for next levelStreak system:- weekly streak = number of consecutive weeks with activity/data entry- monthly streak = number of consecutive months with activity/data entry- current streak- best streak- streak reward bonus XPOptional:- small celebration animation when a streak is maintained- badge icons for consistency milestones--------------------------------------------------PART 4: THEME AND DESIGN--------------------------------------------------Theme:Bali, Indonesia tropical vibesVisual direction:- tropical island feeling- clean and relaxing- palm leaves, ocean tones, sunset colors, warm sand colors- modern and minimal, not cluttered- peaceful but motivating- premium-looking, not childish- subtle tropical gradients- cards should feel fresh, airy, and smooth- the UI should feel like productivity on a beautiful islandColor inspiration:- turquoise / ocean blue- sunset orange- soft palm green- warm beige / sand- white or off-white cards if needed- or a darker tropical sunset mode if it looks betterMake sure:- text is highly readable- numbers are clear- dashboard feels calm and satisfying- mobile layout feels just as good as desktop--------------------------------------------------PART 5: DEVICE SUPPORT--------------------------------------------------Important:The app must work well on both:- PC- iPhoneRequirements:- fully responsive design- mobile-first friendly- easy inputs on iPhone- large tap targets- smooth layout on small screensBest option:- cloud sync / cloud sharing across devices so I can use the same tracker on PC and iPhoneIf cloud sync is used:- use a simple solution like Supabase or Firebase- keep setup simple- make sure data persists across devicesIf cloud sync is too complex:- provide the easiest good fallback- but prioritize cloud sync if possible--------------------------------------------------PART 6: UX REQUIREMENTS--------------------------------------------------Keep it simple.This should not feel bloated.Must have:- one-page dashboard layout- fast input- simple KPI cards- reverse-engineering section- gamification section- clean stats/history section- mobile-friendly navigation if neededNice additions:- save historical KPI snapshots by day, week, or month- weekly summary card- monthly summary card- simple progress chart- export data to CSV- local backup or cloud backup--------------------------------------------------PART 7: TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS--------------------------------------------------Build it as:- responsive HTML/CSS/JavaScript or React- minimal dependencies- clean code- organized file structureProvide:- complete code- file structure- setup instructions- explanation of how KPI calculations work- explanation of how reverse engineering works- explanation of XP, levels, and streak logic- explanation of cloud sync if implemented- explanation of how to run locally--------------------------------------------------IMPORTANT UX DETAILS--------------------------------------------------Please make the app:- simple- smooth- motivating- easy to use daily- visually relaxing- clear on both iPhone and desktopDo not overcomplicate it.Focus on:1. 4 KPI calculations2. reverse engineering3. streaks + XP + levels4. tropical Bali-themed design5. desktop + iPhone usability6. cloud sharing if possible

comment.author: ScottВ Northwolf
comment.content: If your offer is a one liner like "I take self-improvement coaches from $0 to $10K per month in 42 days with my 'Reverse Engineered $10K Method' or they don't pay" then you do tell it even to your neighboor because you lose nothing and create massive curiousity by putting it out there every time you open your mouth.Creating content is the slowest and most inefficient way to get calls but it's the best for the long-term, be prepared to do it consistently and daily if possible for 7 months to 3 years.Cold and warm outreach is the fastest and most powerful way because you get to target precisely your niche, but that, of course, assumes you have locked in PERFECTLY your niche, you know exactly how they look, where they hang out, what's their language and their desired outcome. You need to be able to describe their day to day from the top of your head if I were to ask you at a moment's notice.Running ads with a proven startegy and making less thatn $50K per month is basically insanity, I have seen it a thousand times and some of the best players in the market keep saying it, if you don't have $10K every month that you can literally BURN and forget about them testing different startegies for ads you are throwing money into the trash bin.The ICP is still too broad and doesn't give emotional triggers to the potential audience but it's a good place to start, it's definetely better than nothing.This is the no bullshit answer and I know it sounds harsh but I hope you can take it objectively and analyze these points.I hope it helps, brother.Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brotner! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

original_post.title: Protecting the Focus.
original_post.body: 4LeaВ Newkirk4d вҖўВ Mindset рҹ§ Protecting the Focus.IвҖҷve been reading Deep Work by Cal Newport (recommended by @Scott Northwolf ), and itвҖҷs shifted how I look at the goals weвҖҷve been reverse-engineering together. It made me realize that if we want to hit those big targets we talk about on our Friday calls, we can't afford to have our attention fractured. WeвҖҷre all putting in so much work to stay in a high-frequency state, but IвҖҷve noticed how easily "busy-ness" and multitasking can pull us right back down into the noise. To manifest the version of life weвҖҷre aiming for, IвҖҷm learning that we have to be just as intentional with our focus as we are with our vision.  IвҖҷm curious, whatвҖҷs your take on this? Do you feel like multitasking is a necessary skill for where we're going, or is it just a distraction from the deep work we're actually here to do?12Last comment 2d ago

original_post.title: в—ҖпёҸMoving Backward
original_post.body: 4LeaВ Newkirk5d вҖўВ Holistic Self-Improvement рҹ“ҡв—ҖпёҸMoving BackwardIвҖҷm still sitting with what @Scott Northwolf taught on the call about Reverse Engineering our goals, and honestly, itвҖҷs already changing my life. Instead of looking at where I am now and trying to climb the mountain toward a goal, IвҖҷve been practicing sitting in the frequency of already having achieved it, and then looking back at the steps I took to get there. It shifts everything from "I hope I can do this" to "it is already a done." It makes the path feel so much more inevitable and way less overwhelming.  IвҖҷm curious, whatвҖҷs one goal youвҖҷre currently reverse-engineering? If you look back from the "finished" version of your goal, what was the very first step you took to get there?11Last comment 3d ago

original_post.title: Don't try to escape рҹҳөвҖҚрҹ’«
original_post.body: 5ScottВ Northwolf14d вҖўВ Mindset рҹ§ Don't try to escape рҹҳөвҖҚрҹ’«If you are trying to escape the present moment you are failing in real time.  The masculine error lies on believing that things will be fundamentally different some day.  "Someday my women will stop complaining."  "Someday I'll make enough money and I'll finally be free."  "Someday I'll land a client and everything will change forever."  Guess what?  When you land a client...  You still got to keep doing outreach.  You still got to keep building upwards.  I know because it happened to me.  Don't make the mistake of trying to escape your current situation.  If you stay on this path for long enough you WILL become rich and you WILL miss the days in which you were struggling and striving to hit these goals.  Enjoy the process now.  I know it sounds like a cliche but if you do that and let go of all atatchment to the outcome the inputs will become effortless and everything will flow without frustration.  Delusion backed up just by wo

I love learning and teaching what I learn so I inhale information and make it my job to help people by sharing it. рҹҳӮ 6. Very big on wellness, in every wa

comment.author: ScottВ Northwolf
comment.content: Northwolf... Scott Northwolf.Here's some things about me:Understanding things to a very fundamental level (and I mean EVERYTHING) has always been the highest aim for me since I was a child. I love and get massively inspired by heroic tales of old and great adventures and a long time ago I decided to turn my life into one of those adventures to rival and overshadow Beowulf, Achilles, Hercules, Alexander the Great and a handful of others whom could be considered and are to me the greatest men in human history. I'm deeply passionate about history, philosohpy, comparative religion and mythology, spirituality, the structure of consiousness and the structure of reality itself, human behaviour, science, anthropology and self-improvement. All the above that you just mention. Feels like if you were describing myself... lol рҹҳӮрҹ‘Ң

comment.author: NickВ Banik
comment.content: (edited)My name is Nick BanikI love learning about the mind and how we structure the experiences we have through our perceptions, which is why I love NLP.I am obsessed with resilience and persistence and I strive to become better in anything in interested in. I love learning from the way of the samurai. They were so dynamic in developing mind, body, and spirit through relentless physical training, intellectual studying, creative and artistic development through art, music, and writing, and they connected deeply to God through meditation, prayer, and other practices. I strive to be this way in my own life through playing and writing music, doing dynamic activities like martial arts, snowboarding, trail running, etc, studying and learning things, and practicing my craft all while staying consistent everyday. We can learn so much from the samurai.

original_post.body: 1KamilВ A15d вҖўВ General рҹ’ӯIntroHey, I'm Kamil. I've been on self-improvement for years and became a self-improvement coach this January. original_post.author: ScottВ Northwolf
original_post.title: How does your day looks like?
original_post.body: 5ScottВ Northwolf22d вҖўВ Holistic Self-Improvement рҹ“ҡHow does your day looks like? IвҖҷm curious to knowвҖҰ how does your day looks like?  Mine looks like this: 06:00 Wake up. 06:00 - 07:00 Meditation and gratitude journaling. 07:00 - 08:00 Reading (Principles by Ray Dalio). 08:00 - 09:00 Working out. 09:00 - 09:30 Cold shower. 10:00 - 14:00 Coaching calls. 14:00 - 15:00 Lunch (typically with a friend) 15:00 - 17:00 Deep work (or salesвҖҷ calls). 17:00 - 19:00 Coaching calls. 19:00 - 20:00 Dinner or SalesвҖҷ calls. 20:00 - 21:00 Winding down routine. 21:00 - 21:21 Sleep.  Not judging but if you have a timetable youвҖҷre a legend and got my respect. рҹ«Ў  PS: Yes, the picture is of little houses for roosters. None related at all but I saw them and I thought it was funny as fuck! So there you goвҖҰ here in Bali even roosters are living their best lives with their own private villas. What are you waiting for to join us??? Jack is already comingвҖҰ @Jack Shiller917Last comment 19d ago

comment.author: RobВ NГ¶lken
comment.content: Depends a bit, but usually I wake up with the birds, sometimes meditate but for sure gratitude. Before I go to work I be on Skool and do my emails. Then go to work. Never know what time I am done. In the evening taking a walk, do some more work (website, email and accounting) then hit the bed early . Before I go to sleep I always meditate. Even if there was a party, I always do meditate.

replies.author: ScottВ Northwolf
replies.content: @Rob NГ¶lken I love the flowвҖҰ sounds quite natural and effortless.There are two main modes of approaching your routine (neither is better, they depend on whatвҖҷs your goal and what are you optimizing for):1-Intentional routine (a routine you design yourself with your goals in mind).Pros: you can reverse engineer your goal into actionable steps that you can take every day and itвҖҷs impossible to not hit it with enough time and understanding how to reiterate your process and take in feedback).Cons: It can suffocate inspiration if not properly managed or balanced with seasons of rest.2-Unintentional routine (itвҖҷs basically the routine that forms when we are adapting [effectively and productively or ineffectively and counterproductively] to our environment and internal state).Pros: Great for creativity and inspiration.Cons: It can turn into instant gratification, chaos and too much of a lack of structure that doesnвҖҷt allow for consistency or the creation and maintenance of anything (so goals are quite hard to hit in this way.And of course you can even have a combination of the twoвҖҰ

replies.author: ScottВ Northwolf
replies.content: ThereвҖҷs a LOT to explore more about this subject, but IвҖҷll leave it at that for nowвҖҰ it all has to do with the dynamics between Chaos and Order, Feminine and Masculine energyвҖҰ

original_post.author: AndrewВ Brooks
original_post.title: Food quality in restaurants: probably not for you
original_post.body: 3AndrewВ Brooksрҹ”ҘFeb 24 вҖўВ General рҹ’ӯFood quality in restaurants: probably not for youHow often do you go out, eat somewhere, and think to yourself "well, that wasn't great." And how did you feel afterwards?  There are reasons for the generally low quality of food in most restaurants. Mostly they are limited by their food budget. And the food budget limits the quality they are able to purchase because we also generally want cheap food. What that means is that most of the produce will be conventional produce, not organic. The meats (a large part of the budget) will be of lower quality and the rest will be medium quality at best.  The things we WANT: organic, fresh, healthy, and delicious, simply are not available within the cheap food framework. Unfortunately to get the good stuff you have to pay more. That's just the way it is for now...31Last comment Feb 24

So I'll be DMing you guys perdiodically to jump on one on

original_post.body: 1BrunoВ ManuelFeb 14 вҖўВ General рҹ’ӯYORespect to the tribe.  IвҖҷm focused on self-improvement, building leverage income, and mentoring others to level up body, mind, and finances.  My goal is $10K/month and gain freedom.  For those ahead on the path whatвҖҷs the biggest lesson that accelerated your growth?34Last comment Feb 19

comment.author: ScottВ Northwolf
comment.content: Welcome to the gang, Bruno. Glad to have you with us, brother!One of the biggest things for me was when I stopped thinking about what I wanted and started thinking in terms of what other people wanted and needed.Choosing a niche, going to them and actually asking them consistently and in a methodical way what were their dreams and hopes and what were their biggest problems and using that to build a truly attractive offer that they'd resonated with and they actually wanted to buy and then coming back and offering that to them.And if you picked right the niche and you were able to find them to conduct niche research interviews with them... you'll be able to find them again on the same place and get them on sales' calls with you.Then all that remains is to conduct appointment setting, sales' calls, build a good script and funnel structure and track your KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) in order to improve over time, stay consistent and don't switch niches or business models all together because that resets your progress back to 0.

original_post.author: SajjadВ Bablu
original_post.title: From Content вҶ’ Conversations вҶ’ Real Change (What IвҖҷm Testing Right Now)
original_post.body: 3SajjadВ BabluFeb 14 вҖўВ Holistic Self-Improvement рҹ“ҡFrom Content вҶ’ Conversations вҶ’ Real Change (What IвҖҷm Testing Right Now)Over the last weeks IвҖҷve been studying something interesting:  Most people online are creating content for visibility Very few are creating systems for transformation  Likes donвҖҷt change a person. Motivation videos donвҖҷt change behavior. Even good advice rarely sticks.  So I started experimenting with a different approach.  Instead of posting random self-improvement content, IвҖҷm building a structured flow:  1. A problem people recognize immediately 2. A guided reflection 3. A daily actionable step 4. Consistency over motivation  The goal isnвҖҷt engagement. The goal is clarity + behavior change.  Right now IвҖҷm testing how people respond when the focus shifts from inspiration вҶ’ guidance  Not selling anything here вҖ” just observing behavior patterns and learning what actually helps people follow through in real life.  Curious: Do you think people actually want transformat

comment.author: ScottВ Northwolf
comment.content: This is very interesting, brother. Hope it goes well and please do share what you find out with this approach.Personally I have discovered that people do not value what they get for free for the most part.True transformation I have only seen it taking place through high ticket programs because then they are putting skin on the game, they are committed to make a change and there's real world consecuences to them not doing so (especially if you structure your offer with a powerful full money back conditional guarantee or even a risk reversal one which are usually the best but only when you get to a certain level on the game).This is a clear breakdown of it with some estimate projections that reflect this point:рҹ“Ҡ Success (Completion) Rates by Program TypeProgram TypeTypical Completion Rate (Success Proxy)Free programs~5вҖ“15 % complete Low-ticket programs (e.g., paid but no heavy support)~15вҖ“40 % complete High-ticket programs (e.g., with coaching/community support)~60вҖ“80 %+ completeI actually took the time to dig into this data because I have been seeing this patter for a while and I was wondering if there were any studies about it and there are!It's insane but it's true!Real transformation happens when people are in enough pain so their current situation and staying in it is actually more painful than the effort and sacrifice of taking action consistently and changing radically their lives.I hope understanding this helps you to focus your efforts on the right direction, brother, because I did struggle with this for quite a while, running a lot of free case studies that would end up with VERY high churn and barely any results out of a lack of work and consistency from my mentees but reality is how it is, and I could have sat down and complained about it but instead I decided to accept reality as it is and actually look for the most effective approach to make a meaningful and profound impact on people's lives.If you want to make such impact you either need insane amounts of traffic so this low completion rates get to represent a lot more people than on low traffic enviroments, or you have to switch to a model in which the low traffic is actually very successful because the system is built in such a way that is conductive to VERY high standards and results driven by the level of committment of people (which is what I do and what I personally focus on).Delusion backed up by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brother. Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!

Once the right structure is clear, monetization and commitment layers can be added on top of it.IвҖҷm testing the foundation before scaling the pressure.Will definitely share the results here.

comment.author: AeonВ Bancuyo
comment.content: I have all of this problems

replies.author: ScottВ Northwolf
replies.content: @Aeon Bancuyo alright! So, let's break it down:You are afraid of charging high ticket.Why is that?Is it because you feel like your services are no valuable enough?Then tell me...How much do you feel comfortable with charging (no number is wrong, even if it's 0 at the moment)?Now...whatever that number is...Think...how can you make that number 10 times higher?What kind of MASSIVE value could you provide in order to make it worth that amount of money for people?That's a first step towards reframing this limiting belief.It's a basic framework but it will give you an idea of how much you can improve in order to be able to charge the kind of prices that would not just change your life but your clients' lives as well, because the higher ticket they pay the higher the percieved value they'll see in your offer, the higher their commitment and the higher their likelihood of achieving their desired outcome.The second question is a two part thing.First comes clarity in what you offer and then comes the confidence to state the value behind it.Without that clarity is very hard to express confidently how much are you charging for it, because it's hard to see the value when things are chaotic and confusing and confidence is nothing but a proxy for competence.So we start by actually having a VERY well defined outcome (that aligns with the percieved pain/desired of your prospects) enticing enough for the people in your niche (of course, there are certain foundations to this like picking properly the niche, conducting niche research interviews to actually understand in depth the percieved pain your potential clients have and their desired outcome, but I'll be talking in more depth about the roadmap to $10K per month that we follow here on self-improvement Nation soon to make all of this more clear, I've been asked already a lot for this and it's going to be very useful to our members who haven't spoke to me frequently).With regards to the third one.The fact that you are afraid of paying for mentorship is not really about mentors out there (even though obviusly like in all market there are good ones and shittier ones) but it speaks about how you view yourself and ties back to the fear of having people paying you for your services because you don't believe yet your services are that valuable or perhaps that you can deliever effectively, and if you don't believe (yet) in your own coaching... how can you believe in someone else's?This is just the beginning but I hope it does offers you some clarity and makes you reflect in depth about this subject.I'd recommend deep journaling about it and if you have any questions feel free to reach out, Aeon.Like I said, there's a lot more but take it step by step. It's all about consistency.Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called faith, brother! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

original_post.author: PaulВ Hedron
original_post.title: Basic Leadership Primer
original_post.body: 2PaulВ HedronJan 24 вҖўВ General рҹ’ӯBasic Leadership PrimerThe basic components of a team are as follows:  A group of individuals, a vision, and a facilitator to direct effort and attention.  A leader must be an expert in the nature of the vision, the individuals on the team, and the conditions in which the vision and team interact.  Without a vision or goal, the collection of individuals cannot coalesce. Without individuals to coalesce, a vision or goal is without potential and impetus. Without understanding of the conditions, a team will experience unnecessary inertia, spending effort to achieve the vision.  A vision is merely a blueprint, something to build. The individuals on the team are the builders. The leader is the architect and facilitator.  The base component of a team and shared vision is the individual.  By understanding the individual, a leader has better command of complexity.  An individual has a capable skillset, and limitations. Understanding both the capability/capacity,

comment.author: PaulВ Hedron
comment.content: @Scott Northwolf @Tony Palmer @Nick Steep @Jack Shiller @Flow Z @Andrea PirasLet me know if this is useful.

replies.author: PaulВ Hedron
replies.content: @Scott Northwolf Hey man, yeah, so let's get into this:What I, personally, primarily focus on is producing a "meta-leader"; a leader that produces other leaders.We can look at the basic outline as something like this:Follower - Does what is toldContributor - Contributes autonomouslyCoordinator/Manager - Organizes, controls, and orders workflowLeader - Focus on relationships between individuals and environments/contextsTransformational leader - Focus on growth, development, and capability cultivationSystems leader - Focus on structure and process for scalingMeta-Leader - Leadership development across scales and contextsAnything beyond Meta-Leader is architectural in nature; designing systems and environments where leadership characteristics arise organically, designing systems that produce those systems, designing organized cultural systems that allow for the previously mentioned systems.Beyond that would be existential and civilizational in scale: long-term and broad scopes resulting in overall evolution, and existential emergence. At this scale, think of something like designing an autonomous system where people and individuals could utilize AI to innovate and infinitely scale emergence and emergent properties.I think the natural progression of anyone that gets into self improvement and consulting will inevitably reach some level of meta-leadership, it just doesn't necessarily get noticed.The natural start is transformation leadership; helping an individual essentially express themselves. Not in the way "express yourself" is typically meant; more in an ecological sense. Become the role and ecological niche someone is meant to fill. I started this with alternative therapy, rehabbing "at risk youth" and delinquent adults (criminals, or people at risk of becoming criminal). The individuals I worked with generally had no positive way to integrate into society, so the general gameplan was to identify what they are good at, what they naturally want to do, and find ways to do that, legally, and positively for broader society. A violent guy, for example, is bad in the context of a mugger. In the context of a bouncer or a cop, however, that becomes a useful and sanctioned positive. Same expression of violence, different context.From there, the only natural progressions are focusing on systems that allow for these types of individuals to positively work together, or developing the individuals themselves to be autonomous positive role models.Unfortunately, what I found, and what I was frustrated with, is that the systems that were helping the individuals I liked to work with, also supported individuals that effectively didn't have "real" problems. Weird nerds, derpy kids, people trying to "be cool". Additionally, supporting an individual into being a positive role model requires some level of external support for both the meta-leader, and the individuals they are trying to support. The inevitable outcome of this was cyclical collapse of individuals and systems not properly designed to achieve this.A former violent criminal needs guys that want to live positive lives, and the actual systems, resources, and support to do this.It simply doesn't, or didn't exist at the time.However, the basic skillset is scalable and applicable to other contexts and domains.The tangible outcome; this is basically the same process of self-improvement, extended to its maximum ends. Help an individual achieve mastery in any and every context they exist within, or could exist within.So, in some sense, yes, decentralized cells, or decentralized autonomous organizations(s).In that way, it's not isolated to one tribe, so to speak, but either any tribe, new tribes, or the ability to start tribes.It depends on the context and intent.Specifically, for meta-leadership, that'd mostly be about starting new tribes entirely, or teaching the skills to start a new tribe. From there, you'd have a hierarchy of sub-system leadership that doesn't extend past the boundaries of the tribe.Personally, this is what I do, in some sense or another. I don't align to any one specific group for extended periods, merely because I've identified that there simply needs to be more positive influences and places for people to go.I see this as natural expression at play; if an individual wants to be a leader, then they don't want to be a follower of someone else. They want to express traits of developing their own tribe.At that level, this does start to become more about governing systems and civilizational architecture. So the extension leaves leadership, entirely, and becomes about building something that leaders of various types can actually use and want to use.In some sense, that's also sort of what you're doing, now; uniting and aligning leaders together in a loose coalition or syndicate. Not necessarily all working on the same projects, but multiple projects aligning, together.That's probably more accurate to what I actually do, or attempt to do; help people become someone like you; capable of leading, designing, uniting, and operating, semi-autonomously, but for unified purpose. So, designing systems that produce individuals capable of producing syndicates. That scale is difficult, though, and this type of person is exceptionally rare, and so it naturally defaults down into the meta-leadership level. A syndicate can't form, unless the initial leader is already a meta-leader. That's sort of rare and difficult, but it's certainly doable.So, I just start there.It's also natural default expression for me; I prefer to be around others capable of being entirely independent across scale and context. That would normally be the initial seed of a syndicate, but I simply don't have the interest in sort of stopping, prematurely. The next logical progression would be some level of meta-syndication, creating multiple large aligned organizational networks, and then connecting those networks. That seems to be the scale I want to work towards, but that doesn't necessarily exist, so I can't comment too deeply. I'd have to see it, to know if that's the correct scale I express towards.But yeah, absolutely, different scales and purposes for different people. The same fundamental dynamics exist at every scale, though, so realistically, even imparting any level of leadership capability, even into someone that wants to remain something like a follower or manager, either the natural trait expression exists, or it doesn't. You'd just end up with a super potent, capable follower, with no expression of broader scale leadership interests; they are comfortable being a manager, which is going to operate more effectively than someone who is forced into management, but wants to express natural leader traits. Matching container and component size; a big fish in a small pond, something like that. A lot of solid guys actually prefer to be at the level of follower, merely because there simply aren't guys worth leading, or there aren't things to lead towards. So, it does become a very interesting dynamic to see play out over scales and contexts. That does lead into my fascination with broader architecture; if the conditions existed for broadscale trait expression, what would happen?I simply have no idea, so I simply default down into the meta-leadership layer, because that's about as good as it gets.

comment.author: ScottВ Northwolf
comment.content: When you talk about turning individuals into leaders, do you mean creating something like individual autonomous cells, like a decentralized organization?The thing is, I believe this can be either interpreted as giving enough understanding and power to each individual so they develop themselves and become truly useful members for the tribe OR as pseudo-anarchic kind of organization, a decentralized thing, kind of like the internet phenomenon of Anonymous.With the first interpretation, building upwards members of the tribe and getting them to a place of taking absolute personal responsibility for their lives, being capable of autonomously and sustainably grow and have their own systems in place for their development and for yielding consistently results for themselves and for moving forwards the cause of the tribeвҖҰ thatвҖҷs powerful and aligns with my vision and naturally will always align with the vision of any self-improvement mentor.But decentralized structures and multiple cells of command only work for very specific kinds of purposes and environments.I donвҖҷt believe we have the need to over-think all this at the moment.Things like culture, organization and logistics and politics tend to be better put into practice by empirical testing and exploration, especially at the beginning stages.

original_post.author: AeonВ Bancuyo
original_post.title: What do you do to keep being social?
original_post.body: 2AeonВ BancuyoJan 28 вҖўВ General рҹ’ӯWhat do you do to keep being social?IвҖҷm curious do you have friends or do you interact often with people not on self improvement?  I thought about the picture of the about page that @Scott Northwolf has, and I would love to have that kind of friend group as well.  Honestly, IвҖҷve been in pain my whole life for not having a вҖңstableвҖқ friend group, is this normal?  My friends circle weвҖҷre only 3 at a time, and it would die off overtime.  Currently, having not deep relationships with my school friends.  ThereвҖҷs so many people who I can be friends with in school, itвҖҷs too easy.  But I have a limiting belief that, theyre not worth it. Honestly, I get the вҖңickвҖқ when IвҖҷm trying to fit in to the gossip conversations, or the shallow вҖңissueвҖқ of the day.  What do you do to battle this, I have a two friends that I have a deep relationship with but not at school.  What do you do to keep being social, even with people outside of self imorovemnt?35Last comment Jan 29

comment.author: ScottВ Northwolf
comment.content: I'm not really social with people who are not into self-improvement to be honest. The beauty about Bali is that EVERYONE (or at least most people) are into self-improvement so that makes it perfect but I know this is a very rare place, that's also the reason I moved here.Back in the day, when I was still back home I had a big group of friends but when I got into self-improvement I just stopped having things in common with them, our interests just weren't the same and even though I tried to get them on self-improvement I failed. You cannot force people to improve their lives if they don't want to.So basically I endured the time in solitude and used it to not be afraid of being alone and carve my own path, I also used it as motivation to make a shit ton of money and start traveling the world in order to find my tribe which I successfully did here in Bali.On self-improvement you'll go through this phase of loneliness in which you are not high status enough yet to be friends with the people you want to be friends in real life because they are still way above you but if you use that as motivation to get to their level you will get there, it's just a matter of time and commitment.I know it's hard but it's worth it and there's no going back once you take the red pill anyways.The only option is to keep moving forward, brother. Use this community to remind yourself of the people you'll have around you when you make it to the other side and don't lose faith.A lot of us are living here already and we have fulfilled the dream we were shooting for... now we are building together towards a higher aim and I can tell you there's nothing better than making your dreams come true with effort, sacrifice and dedication.

replies.author: AeonВ Bancuyo
replies.content: @Scott Northwolf thanks for this man!I'll keep this as a reminder, but do you think there's a way for me to be not a social nerd and just make myself isolated, whilst on self improvement?

replies.author: ScottВ Northwolf
replies.content: @Aeon Bancuyo the funny thing about social skills is that you can actually develop them more by developing yourself and creating a rock solid confidence born out of REAL competence than by simply "doing reps" and talking to normies non-stop. It's the highest leverage thing you can do. By developing this sense of pride on yourself and having high standards for the people around you, counter-intuitively you become magnetic to people. I know the instant gratification pull of being social is strong but I can promise you that locking in and developing yourself as a man will only bring net benefits to your life and will not turn you into a "social weirdo" or a "self-improvement nerd" umless you hold such limiting belief and turn it into a self fulfilling profecy.

comment.author: AndrewВ Brooks
comment.content: I'm not really "social" either. I have about 3 good friends in my locale, and I have others that I connect with virtually every week. But I do feel the need to be social often.So, I invite friends over for dinner, plant to meet up for a coffee or a movie, or just meet up to hang out somewhere for an hour. It doesn't have to be a big thing.But I do plan fun things intentionally. I keep track of the artsy/cultural things happening in my area and will often just show up to some of those and see if I can chat with a new person there. And I will sometimes invite people I meet to connect with me at the event. Sometimes they show, sometimes not. But I'm ok doing things by myself too.So don't feel weird. You aren't. Especially as we get older, the number of friends typically dwindles, but the QUALITY of the friendships goes up. And that's ok.

replies.author: ScottВ Northwolf
replies.content: @Andrew Brooks very grounded approach.

original_post.title: Be full of yourself, be delusional.
original_post.body: 2Laurits ValentinВ Offersenрҹ”ҘJan 21 вҖўВ Mindset рҹ§ Be full of yourself, be delusional.IвҖҷm 18 years old and IвҖҷm fully aware that, on paper, IвҖҷm not that impressive yet.  I donвҖҷt have crazy money.IвҖҷm not a finished product.IвҖҷm still early.  But hereвҖҷs the part most people wonвҖҷt admit:  IвҖҷm delusional about where IвҖҷm going.  Not in an ego way, in a commitment way.I know IвҖҷm getting there because IвҖҷve already decided I wonвҖҷt stop.  That belief is what gets me up at 5 am  That belief is what keeps me working when no oneвҖҷs watching.  That belief is what makes quitting impossible.  People call it arrogance.  I call it certainty.  If I didnвҖҷt believe that strongly in my future self, IвҖҷd move like everyone else, procrastinating, Hesitating and Half-committed.  But I donвҖҷt need proof yet.  I just need to stay consistent.  Being вҖңdelusionalвҖқ is only a problem when you donвҖҷt work.When you do the work, it becomes inevitable.  if you do average ammounts of work you become average  if you do exceptional

Since 8 years old due to parents and family giving me digital games always more and more advanced, starting from their will to let me have fun until it became my addiction and I imposed my will to have more games. Cellphone, porn, Music, Junk food. One day in 2022 Hamza knocked in my youtube algorithm. Started working on myself. Reached higher and higher status, improved my skills, improved my mental health dramatically, and all the other benefits of self-improvement. Nowadays I'm a coach and teach other people how to gain back control of themselves.  2. What was the pain that got you into self-improvement? No social status, insecureness, and sense of emptiness due to addictions  3. How did you get into self-improvement coaching/consulting? I had a calling to help other people do the same transformation as I did  4. How long have you'

original_post.body: 5ScottВ NorthwolfNov '25 вҖўВ Holistic Self-Improvement рҹ“ҡThe three components of the Meta-skill рҹҺҜ1. Deliberate Practice. 2. Reflection upon your practice. 3. Theoretical knowledge applied to your practice.  Deliberate practice:  To become a grand master at chess the first thing you do is start playing. Once you have been playing for a while you will reach a certain level of skill. LetвҖҷs call it level 1. You have completed level 1 of that skill (obviously this is a simplistic way of seeing it, itвҖҷs hard to pinpoint theoretically at which point you have actually achieved the highest level of skill you could obtain from practice alone, but combined with the other components of the meta-skill you can advance way faster). Then youвҖҷll realize that to keep getting better youвҖҷll need something else. YouвҖҷll hit a ceiling. Then comes the next levelвҖҰ  Reflection upon your practice:  If you start analyzing every single move you make, write it down, pay attention to every move your opponent makes, tr

original_post.body: 5ScottВ NorthwolfNov '25 вҖўВ Mindset рҹ§ How to Set Goals Like a Millionaire (and actually achieve them)The first thing you need to realize is that if you don't have a goal that is your north star you are like a ship in the middle of the ocean, what surrounds you is the uncertain, chaos, and until you have a goal, you will not know what's the actual direction you want to go, what's your port of destination, with a destination you can build order out of chaos. Thinking from first principles is one of the most powerful things you can do, setting your goal through a process of deep journaling and profound inquiry is the thing that will bring the most clarity in your life. So... how do we go about setting ourselves a proper goal? First we ask to ourselves "What do I want out of life?" Chances are, if you are on this community you want to make money online. But what really means to make money online? Being an educational content creator? What it means is freedom. We want freedom to be able to ch

replies.author: ScottВ Northwolf
replies.content: @Axel Lionel think about it like this: You're getting girls right now but... are they the highest quality ever? Not even 10 out of 10s but 20 out of fucking 10s? If it's a yes are they easy to get? Easy enough that you feel like you have a boundless stream of them?What I mean by this is...ALWAYS choose delayed gratification.If you give up the 8s and the 9s now and focus on keep improving relentlessly yourself, on building a legacy and becoming the most powerful version of yourself you could ever be...Then you'll get a thousand times better women than what you're getting now.Think about it deeply to yourself. Journal about it. Be truthful and honest with yourself. Don't cope.In the end the pain of regret is always going to be stronger than the pain of meeting resistance with relentless effort and trying with everything you have.We, as men deeply in love with GREATNESS, are blessed and cursed with awareness. Awareness of our true potential. And we shall not be able to truly rest until we have done everything within our power to meet such potential and manifest it into reality.When you realize these things and adopt this mindset...Distractions become SOOOOOOO MUCH easier to overcome.Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called faith, brother. Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!

original_post.author: BrandonВ Maloney
original_post.title: Does anyone know how to become ambitious?
original_post.body:
6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ9d  вҖў рҹӨқNetworkingDoes anyone know how to become ambitious?I've realized recently I'm not obsessed like I used to be with things. I would say I'm more productive, smarter, more skilled, but the absolute OBSESSION I had with sports, or training, or work, just simply isn't there to the degree it used to be at the moment.My question to you is, how do you WANT more. How do you give yourself big goals that almost scare you, that make you crave working on them?(Also funny picture of my when I was in middle school)
    To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar.
    While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item.
    Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.
  Like2847 comments5ZakhВ Chowdhury вҖў 9dI've consumed 100s of books, podcasts, articles, and courses on neuroscience, metaphyiscs, psychology, and manifestation and here is my viewpoint on developing ambition:Most people think they lack ambition, but what they actually lack is clarity, and those are two very different problems. Ambition isnвҖҷt something you force, itвҖҷs something that naturally shows up when youвҖҷre moving toward the right thing. The issue is that most of us are chasing goals that were never really ours in the first place. TheyвҖҷre inherited from social media, family expectations, or whatever environment weвҖҷve been in, so even if they sound good on paper, they feel heavy when you actually try to pursue them. ThatвҖҷs why you can feel stuck, unmotivated, or constantly second guessing yourself. ItвҖҷs not because youвҖҷre lazy, itвҖҷs because thereвҖҷs a misalignment between what youвҖҷre chasing and what actually resonates with you.The first thing you have to do is remove pressure. When you tell yourself you need to figure everything out or you have to be successful, you create this internal tension that actually blocks clarity. You start overthinking, comparing, and forcing decisions instead of seeing things clearly. When you loosen that grip and shift into a mindset of choosing rather than needing, things start to open up. You think better, you notice more, and youвҖҷre not operating from fear. From there, you need to separate what you genuinely want from what you think you should want. A simple way to do that is to ask yourself if no one could see your life, no recognition, no validation, would you still choose this path? If the answer is no, then itвҖҷs probably not your goal. Another good check is how it feels when you think about it. The right direction usually feels energizing, even if itвҖҷs challenging. The wrong one feels draining, even if itвҖҷs achievable.Once you start filtering things this way, you can begin building a clear vision, not just of outcomes, but of the type of person you want to be and the kind of life you want to live day to day. Most people get stuck because they only think in terms of results, like money or status, but clarity actually comes from seeing yourself in motion, your habits, your environment, your work, your lifestyle. When that picture becomes clear, your brain starts recognizing opportunities that align with it, and decisions become easier because youвҖҷre not guessing anymore. At that point, instead of relying purely on discipline, you start following energy. Pay attention to what you naturally gravitate toward, what you think about without forcing it, what feels meaningful even when itвҖҷs hard. ThatвҖҷs usually where your real path is. It might not look logical at first, but itвҖҷs where consistency becomes effortless because youвҖҷre not fighting yourself.Ambition, then, isnвҖҷt about grinding harder or pushing yourself endlessly. ItвҖҷs about locking into a direction that pulls you forward. When youвҖҷre aligned, you donвҖҷt need constant motivation, you donвҖҷt question everything, and you donвҖҷt feel like youвҖҷre forcing your life to happen. Things start to click because your actions, your thoughts, and your direction are all working together instead of against each other. So if you feel stuck right now, donвҖҷt try to become more ambitious. Focus on getting clear. Strip away the noise, remove the pressure, and start paying attention to what actually feels true for you. Because the right goals donвҖҷt need to be chased, they create their own momentum, and once you find those, ambition stops being something you struggle with and becomes something you naturally embody.Lmaoooo sorry for the long wordy post but hopefully this helps anyone that sees it :)6Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Zakh Chowdhury thank you a ton for the feedback, this was honestly a difficult read for me because I'm a lil slow, but here's what I got from it:Find out what you wantFind the clearest path to get itBe authenticLet your pulls pull youDoes that sound accurate?1Reply5ZakhВ Chowdhury вҖў 8d@Brandon Maloney sounds good to me!1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 7d@Zakh Chowdhury Thank you, I'll put that into actoin0Reply7BillyВ Harcourt вҖў 9dYou've got to have a strong enough whyI'd recommend reading 'Start with why' by Simon SinekWhen your why is strong enough, you feel a compulsive need to work on the things you have great ambitions about4Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 9d@Billy Harcourt thank you mate0Reply7HarryВ Montfordрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 9dWhat worked for me was increasing my intrinsic motivation. It might sound super weird but this is usually what happens when we try to reach our goals:Extrinsic motivation sparks action вҶ’ Action sparks flow вҶ’ Flow boosts intrinsic motivation.Extrinsic motivation is things outside of us like money fame etc. When we take action, that puts us in flow state and that boosts our intrinsic motivation which is our internal reasons for doing something.What really helped me increase my intrinsic motivation is having clear goals and a clear why like the above comments said as well :)1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Harry Montford honestly IвҖҷm not smart enough to understand what you mean, can you dumb it down a little bit like IвҖҷm extra slow?0Reply6ScottВ Northwolf вҖў 8d3 things:1-Goal setting, drive vacuums identification and reverse engineering of your goal into actionable steps.2-After identifying your goal get inspiration and information about it (e.g. if you want to become a millionaire read the biographies of Andrew Carnegie and Rockefeller, if you want to become a military genious read Alexander's Campagins by Arian and Napoleon: A life).3-Identify your current beliefs with regards to the components of reality that you are struggling with and flip those beliefs in their head, build then a Self-Transcendence Document or Alchemy of Self Workbook.If you want me to explain in more depth those things just shoot me a DM. I know the third one is the "weirdest" one but it requires a LOT of explanation so it makes sense...The logical chain is like this:Experiences form beliefs.Beliefs form your identity.Your identity informs your paradigm (the lenses through which you see the world and interpret it).Your paradigm will inform your percieved options.Your percieved options will inform your actions (you can only make a decision out of the percieved options you believe are available to you).Your actions will solidify (only temporarily) into your behaviour (habits, routines [intentional or unintentional]).Your behaviour will determine your results.1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Scott Northwolf Thank you for taking the time to write all of this, I do appriciate it.So in short:Have a goalSurround yourself with inspirationMake the actions required as automatic as possibleAm I getting that right?1Reply6ScottВ Northwolf вҖў 8d@Brandon Maloney the actionable steps are broken down in a lot more detail in my community, you might want to check that out later, bro.1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 7d@Scott Northwolf appriciate it1Reply4SioneВ Lavulavuрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8dFor me it starts with deletion. Delete, delete, delete distractions.I commit to, focus on and stay consistent with what matters most.This has helped me. Refocusing on the main thing revealed my true mission and purpose in life.Now I pursue with clarity and ambition.Only thing left is execution.1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Sione Lavulavu so you'd say to just remove as many options as possible?1Reply4SioneВ Lavulavuрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Brandon Maloney Yes, options are distractions.When I'm not ambitious it's because I'm not clear on what I truly want. I need clarity in those moments.If your Mom was going to get shot in front of you by someone, you would have clarity on what to do next.Most times we know what we need to do, we're just afraid to do it.Delete delete delete distractions.1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Sione Lavulavu I see, that's a very good insight, thank you.1Reply4SioneВ Lavulavuрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Brandon Maloney Go take down that fool trying to figuratively take your dreams, most times it's just us.I'm the problem and the solution.Synthesizer classroom will set you straight.Get at itрҹӨҳрҹӨҷ1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Sione Lavulavu Thanks Sione :)1Reply4SioneВ Lavulavuрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Brandon Maloney FYI, I fight with this daily, so this is just my own experience.1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Sione Lavulavu of course, thank you again1Reply3MichelleВ Martiya вҖў 8dAmbition isnвҖҷt really a necessary part of the equation, I donвҖҷt think. IвҖҷm not really ambitious, but I do love teaching, so IвҖҷd like to do more of that.Procrastination, howeverвҖҰ now thatвҖҷs a whole other issue that often gets in the way. Maybe itвҖҷs less of a вҖңbe more ambitiousвҖқ problem and more of a вҖңless procrastinationвҖқ problem. рҹӨ·рҹҸ»вҖҚвҷҖпёҸ1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Michelle Martiya Maybe, I see what you're getting at.The thing is I WANT to want to be more ambitious and set my sights higher. Just right now I haven't. I used to want to be in the olympics n'shit when I was younger (and was honestly on track haha), now I'm a medicore feeling fitness coach who doesn't have that "I want to change the world!" mentality I used to have. I want it back, that's why I was asking :(0Reply3MichelleВ Martiya вҖў 8d@Brandon Maloney Ohhh yeah, I feel that. IвҖҷm in the same place, actually. When I started training animals, I really wanted to вҖңchange the worldвҖқ as you said. Now I just want to work with people who already are in line with my philosophies. IвҖҷm afraid I donвҖҷt have an answer for you.1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 8d@Michelle Martiya No problem, what animals do you train? That's pretty cool0Reply3MichelleВ Martiya вҖў 8d@Brandon Maloney all of them! Except birds and most primates. Those I refer to other trainers.1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 7d@Michelle Martiya that's really freaking cool :OWhat do you teach them?1Reply3MichelleВ Martiya вҖў 7d@Brandon Maloney depends on the species. With dogs, I do everything from basic puppy training to serious behavior issues like reactivity and aggression. With equine spaces I work mostly with fear and trauma cases. With exotics itвҖҷs usually just about getting them to be manageable and friendly.Animals all learn the same way so the differences lie solely in what people want to teach them. The underlying theme is teaching people how to consciously and effectively communicate with their animals to get the behaviors they want.1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 7d@Michelle Martiya wait that's so cool! That would have been my next question on if all animals were trained the same.Is it similar to human conditioning as well!??!?!?!?!?2Reply3MichelleВ Martiya вҖў 7d@Brandon Maloney itвҖҷs exactly the same рҹҳҮ1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 6d@Michelle Martiya that's so neat! How can I condition my friends to sit and paw when I want them to? (Genuinely how would you go about that as a fun thought experiement)0Reply3MichelleВ Martiya вҖў 6d@Brandon Maloney youвҖҷll have to expand on that a littleвҖҰ sit could be human or many other species. Paw is usually reserved for animals рҹӨ·рҹҸ»вҖҚвҷҖпёҸ1Reply6BrandonВ Maloneyрҹ”Ҙ вҖў 6d@Michelle Martiya no no no...I need to make my friends give me paw, it's crucial for the safety of the human race.HYPOTHETICALLY speaking, how COULD you condition someone to do those things (I'm not saying realistically, just ideallistically y'know? рҹҳҠ)0Reply3MichelleВ Martiya вҖў 6d@Brandon Maloney Many trainers like to play what we call the "clicker game" with their human learners to help them understand how animals learn.So pick a reinforcer (Skittles, M&M's, quarters, etc.). Give your human learner a cup. Have them move randomly around the room. Whenever they get any approximation towards the goal behavior (moving toward the chair for "sit"), put a reinforcer in their cup. Keep reinforcing each small approximation until they figure out that you want them to sit. This is positive reinforcement. Alternately (don't do this unless you've done #1 first, or your human learner will quit), you can pick a sound that's super annoying, like fingernails on a chalkboard. Play the sound continuously until your human learner gets an approximation towards the goal behavior. After a second or two, start playing the sound again until they get another approximation. Continue until they figure out that you want them to sit. This is negative reinforcement.You can use the same sound, but this time, whenever your human learner gets the wrong answer (moves away from the chair), play the sound briefly. Keep doing this until they figure out that you want them to sit in the chair. Caution: your friend may hate you after this one. This is positive punishment. Lastly, fill your human learner's cup with one of those reinforcers from the first lesson. Every time they get the wrong answer, take away a few reinforcers. Keep doing this until they figure out that you want them to sit. This is negative punishment. «рҹ“Әрҹ“¬рҹ“ӯрҹ“®рҹ—іпёҸвңҸпёҸвң’пёҸрҹ–ӢпёҸрҹ–ҠпёҸрҹ–ҢпёҸрҹ–ҚпёҸрҹ“қрҹ’јрҹ“Ғрҹ“Ӯрҹ—ӮпёҸрҹ“…рҹ“Ҷрҹ—’пёҸрҹ—“пёҸрҹ“Үрҹ“Ҳрҹ“үрҹ“Ҡрҹ“Ӣрҹ“Ңрҹ“Қрҹ“Һрҹ–ҮпёҸрҹ“Ҹрҹ“җвңӮпёҸрҹ—ғпёҸрҹ—„пёҸрҹ—‘пёҸрҹ”’рҹ”“рҹ”Ҹрҹ”җрҹ”‘рҹ—қпёҸрҹ”ЁрҹӘ“вӣҸпёҸвҡ’пёҸрҹӣ пёҸрҹ—ЎпёҸвҡ”пёҸрҹ”«рҹӘғрҹҸ№рҹӣЎпёҸрҹӘҡрҹ”§рҹӘӣрҹ”©вҡҷпёҸрҹ—ңпёҸвҡ–пёҸрҹҰҜрҹ”—вӣ“пёҸрҹӘқрҹ§°рҹ§ІрҹӘңвҡ—пёҸрҹ§Әрҹ§«рҹ§¬рҹ”¬рҹ”ӯрҹ“Ўрҹ’үрҹ©ёрҹ’Ҡрҹ©№рҹ©јрҹ©әрҹ©»рҹҡӘрҹӣ—рҹӘһрҹӘҹрҹӣҸпёҸрҹӣӢпёҸрҹӘ‘рҹҡҪрҹӘ рҹҡҝрҹӣҒрҹӘӨрҹӘ’рҹ§ҙрҹ§·рҹ§№рҹ§әрҹ§»рҹӘЈрҹ§јрҹ«§рҹӘҘрҹ§Ҫрҹ§Ҝрҹӣ’рҹҡ¬вҡ°пёҸрҹӘҰвҡұпёҸрҹ—ҝрҹӘ§рҹӘӘSymbolsрҹҸ§рҹҡ®рҹҡ°вҷҝрҹҡ№рҹҡәрҹҡ»рҹҡјрҹҡҫрҹӣӮрҹӣғрҹӣ„рҹӣ…вҡ пёҸрҹҡёвӣ”рҹҡ«рҹҡірҹҡӯрҹҡҜрҹҡұрҹҡ·рҹ“өрҹ”һвҳўпёҸвҳЈпёҸв¬ҶпёҸвҶ—пёҸвһЎпёҸвҶҳпёҸв¬ҮпёҸвҶҷпёҸв¬…пёҸвҶ–пёҸвҶ•пёҸвҶ”пёҸвҶ©пёҸвҶӘпёҸвӨҙпёҸвӨөпёҸрҹ”ғрҹ”„рҹ”ҷрҹ”ҡрҹ”ӣрҹ”ңрҹ”қрҹӣҗвҡӣпёҸрҹ•үпёҸвңЎпёҸвҳёпёҸвҳҜпёҸвңқпёҸвҳҰпёҸвҳӘпёҸвҳ®пёҸрҹ•Һрҹ”ҜвҷҲвҷүвҷҠвҷӢвҷҢвҷҚвҷҺвҷҸвҷҗвҷ‘вҷ’вҷ“вӣҺрҹ”Җрҹ”Ғрҹ”Ӯв–¶пёҸвҸ©вҸӯпёҸвҸҜпёҸв—ҖпёҸвҸӘвҸ®пёҸрҹ”јвҸ«рҹ”ҪвҸ¬вҸёпёҸвҸ№пёҸвҸәпёҸвҸҸпёҸрҹҺҰрҹ”…рҹ”Ҷрҹ“¶рҹ“ірҹ“ҙвҷҖпёҸвҷӮпёҸвҡ§пёҸвң–пёҸвһ•вһ–вһ—рҹҹ°вҷҫпёҸвҖјпёҸвҒүпёҸвқ“вқ”вқ•вқ—гҖ°пёҸрҹ’ұрҹ’Івҡ•пёҸвҷ»пёҸвҡңпёҸрҹ”ұрҹ“ӣрҹ”°вӯ•вң…вҳ‘пёҸвң”пёҸвқҢвқҺвһ°вһҝгҖҪпёҸвңіпёҸвңҙпёҸвқҮпёҸВ©пёҸВ®пёҸв„ўпёҸ#пёҸвғЈ*пёҸвғЈ0пёҸвғЈ1пёҸвғЈ2пёҸвғЈ3пёҸвғЈ4пёҸвғЈ5пёҸвғЈ6пёҸвғЈ7пёҸвғЈ8пёҸвғЈ9пёҸвғЈрҹ”ҹрҹ” рҹ”Ўрҹ”ўрҹ”Јрҹ”Өрҹ…°пёҸрҹҶҺрҹ…ұпёҸрҹҶ‘рҹҶ’рҹҶ“в„№пёҸрҹҶ”в“ӮпёҸрҹҶ•рҹҶ–рҹ…ҫпёҸрҹҶ—рҹ…ҝпёҸрҹҶҳрҹҶҷрҹҶҡрҹҲҒрҹҲӮпёҸрҹҲ·пёҸрҹҲ¶рҹҲҜрҹүҗрҹҲ№рҹҲҡрҹҲІрҹү‘рҹҲёрҹҲҙрҹҲігҠ—пёҸгҠҷпёҸрҹҲәрҹҲөрҹ”ҙрҹҹ рҹҹЎрҹҹўрҹ”өрҹҹЈрҹҹӨвҡ«вҡӘрҹҹҘрҹҹ§рҹҹЁрҹҹ©рҹҹҰрҹҹӘрҹҹ«в¬ӣв¬ңв—јпёҸв—»пёҸв—ҫв—Ҫв–ӘпёҸв–«пёҸрҹ”¶рҹ”·рҹ”ёрҹ”№рҹ”әрҹ”»рҹ’ рҹ”ҳрҹ”ірҹ”ІFlagsрҹҸҒрҹҡ©рҹҺҢрҹҸҙрҹҸіпёҸрҹҸіпёҸвҖҚрҹҢҲрҹҸіпёҸвҖҚвҡ§пёҸрҹҸҙвҖҚвҳ пёҸрҹҮҰрҹҮЁрҹҮҰрҹҮ©рҹҮҰрҹҮӘрҹҮҰрҹҮ«рҹҮҰрҹҮ¬рҹҮҰрҹҮ®рҹҮҰрҹҮұрҹҮҰрҹҮІрҹҮҰрҹҮҙрҹҮҰрҹҮ¶рҹҮҰрҹҮ·рҹҮҰрҹҮёрҹҮҰрҹҮ№рҹҮҰрҹҮәрҹҮҰрҹҮјрҹҮҰрҹҮҪрҹҮҰрҹҮҝрҹҮ§рҹҮҰрҹҮ§рҹҮ§рҹҮ§рҹҮ©рҹҮ§рҹҮӘрҹҮ§рҹҮ«рҹҮ§рҹҮ¬рҹҮ§рҹҮӯрҹҮ§рҹҮ®рҹҮ§рҹҮҜрҹҮ§рҹҮұрҹҮ§рҹҮІрҹҮ§рҹҮірҹҮ§рҹҮҙрҹҮ§рҹҮ¶рҹҮ§рҹҮ·рҹҮ§рҹҮёрҹҮ§рҹҮ№рҹҮ§рҹҮ»рҹҮ§рҹҮјрҹҮ§рҹҮҫрҹҮ§рҹҮҝрҹҮЁрҹҮҰрҹҮЁрҹҮЁрҹҮЁрҹҮ©рҹҮЁрҹҮ«рҹҮЁрҹҮ¬рҹҮЁрҹҮӯрҹҮЁрҹҮ®рҹҮЁрҹҮ comment.author: ZakhВ Chowdhury
comment.content: I've consumed 100s of books, podcasts, articles, and courses on neuroscience, metaphyiscs, psychology, and manifestation and here is my viewpoint on developing ambition:Most people think they lack ambition, but what they actually lack is clarity, and those are two very different problems. Ambition isnвҖҷt something you force, itвҖҷs something that naturally shows up when youвҖҷre moving toward the right thing. The issue is that most of us are chasing goals that were never really ours in the first place. TheyвҖҷre inherited from social media, family expectations, or whatever environment weвҖҷve been in, so even if they sound good on paper, they feel heavy when you actually try to pursue them. ThatвҖҷs why you can feel stuck, unmotivated, or constantly second guessing yourself. ItвҖҷs not because youвҖҷre lazy, itвҖҷs because thereвҖҷs a misalignment between what youвҖҷre chasing and what actually resonates with you.The first thing you have to do is remove pressure. When you tell yourself you need to figure everything out or you have to be successful, you create this internal tension that actually blocks clarity. You start overthinking, comparing, and forcing decisions instead of seeing things clearly. When you loosen that grip and shift into a mindset of choosing rather than needing, things start to open up. You think better, you notice more, and youвҖҷre not operating from fear. From there, you need to separate what you genuinely want from what you think you should want. A simple way to do that is to ask yourself if no one could see your life, no recognition, no validation, would you still choose this path? If the answer is no, then itвҖҷs probably not your goal. Another good check is how it feels when you think about it. The right direction usually feels energizing, even if itвҖҷs challenging. The wrong one feels draining, even if itвҖҷs achievable.Once you start filtering things this way, you can begin building a clear vision, not just of outcomes, but of the type of person you want to be and the kind of life you want to live day to day. Most people get stuck because they only think in terms of results, like money or status, but clarity actually comes from seeing yourself in motion, your habits, your environment, your work, your lifestyle. When that picture becomes clear, your brain starts recognizing opportunities that align with it, and decisions become easier because youвҖҷre not guessing anymore. At that point, instead of relying purely on discipline, you start following energy. Pay attention to what you naturally gravitate toward, what you think about without forcing it, what feels meaningful even when itвҖҷs hard. ThatвҖҷs usually where your real path is. It might not look logical at first, but itвҖҷs where consistency becomes effortless because youвҖҷre not fighting yourself.Ambition, then, isnвҖҷt about grinding harder or pushing yourself endlessly. ItвҖҷs about locking into a direction that pulls you forward. When youвҖҷre aligned, you donвҖҷt need constant motivation, you donвҖҷt question everything, and you donвҖҷt feel like youвҖҷre forcing your life to happen. Things start to click because your actions, your thoughts, and your direction are all working together instead of against each other. So if you feel stuck right now, donвҖҷt try to become more ambitious. Focus on getting clear. Strip away the noise, remove the pressure, and start paying attention to what actually feels true for you. Because the right goals donвҖҷt need to be chased, they create their own momentum, and once you find those, ambition stops being something you struggle with and becomes something you naturally embody.Lmaoooo sorry for the long wordy post but hopefully this helps anyone that sees it :)

comment.author: HarryВ Montford
comment.content: What worked for me was increasing my intrinsic motivation. It might sound super weird but this is usually what happens when we try to reach our goals:Extrinsic motivation sparks action вҶ’ Action sparks flow вҶ’ Flow boosts intrinsic motivation.Extrinsic motivation is things outside of us like money fame etc. When we take action, that puts us in flow state and that boosts our intrinsic motivation which is our internal reasons for doing something.What really helped me increase my intrinsic motivation is having clear goals and a clear why like the above comments said as well :)

comment.author: ScottВ Northwolf
comment.content: 3 things:1-Goal setting, drive vacuums identification and reverse engineering of your goal into actionable steps.2-After identifying your goal get inspiration and information about it (e.g. if you want to become a millionaire read the biographies of Andrew Carnegie and Rockefeller, if you want to become a military genious read Alexander's Campagins by Arian and Napoleon: A life).3-Identify your current beliefs with regards to the components of reality that you are struggling with and flip those beliefs in their head, build then a Self-Transcendence Document or Alchemy of Self Workbook.If you want me to explain in more depth those things just shoot me a DM. I know the third one is the "weirdest" one but it requires a LOT of explanation so it makes sense...The logical chain is like this:Experiences form beliefs.Beliefs form your identity.Your identity informs your paradigm (the lenses through which you see the world and interpret it).Your paradigm will inform your percieved options.Your percieved options will inform your actions (you can only make a decision out of the percieved options you believe are available to you).Your actions will solidify (only temporarily) into your behaviour (habits, routines [intentional or unintentional]).Your behaviour will determine your results.

replies.author: MichelleВ Martiya
replies.content:
@Brandon Maloney Many trainers like to play what we call the "clicker game" with their human learners to help them understand how animals learn.So pick a reinforcer (Skittles, M&M's, quarters, etc.). Give your human learner a cup. Have them move randomly around the room. Whenever they get any approximation towards the goal behavior (moving toward the chair for "sit"), put a reinforcer in their cup. Keep reinforcing each small approximation until they figure out that you want them to sit. This is positive reinforcement. Alternately (don't do this unless you've done #1 first, or your human learner will quit), you can pick a sound that's super annoying, like fingernails on a chalkboard. Play the sound continuously until your human learner gets an approximation towards the goal behavior. After a second or two, start playing the sound again until they get another approximation. Continue until they figure out that you want them to sit. This is negative reinforcement.You can use the same sound, but this time, whenever your human learner gets the wrong answer (moves away from the chair), play the sound briefly. Keep doing this until they figure out that you want them to sit in the chair. Caution: your friend may hate you after this one. This is positive punishment. Lastly, fill your human learner's cup with one of those reinforcers from the first lesson. Every time they get the wrong answer, take away a few reinforcers. Keep doing this until they figure out that you want them to sit. This is negative punishment. Good luck, and I'm gonna need video of all of this! рҹҳ…Here's an example of the first one: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c1eda0XmoLkNobody ever demos the others because they make human learners feel bad, which is ironic, since those are the ones people use most with their animals.2:05
